narrative: As an Asian male applicant, your law school application was evaluated using a predictive model that assesses the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try, based on academic records, test scores, and background details like yours. The model estimated a 56.6% probability that you would fail the bar exam, which crossed the threshold for rejection, as the school prioritizes admitting students predicted to succeed right away. The primary driver pushing toward this failure prediction was your Year 3 school decile ranking of 2, which placed you in the lower half of your peers—well below the typical student's decile of around 5.5—suggesting inconsistent performance later in your studies that the model sees as a red flag for bar success. Counterbalancing this somewhat was your undergraduate GPA of 3.30, which is stronger than the average of 3.21 and pulled the prediction toward passing by showing solid prior academic ability. Your upper-middle family income also nudged the outlook slightly toward failure, as the model associates certain income brackets with outcomes less favorable than average. While your LSAT score of 34 is below the typical 36.7 and your full-time enrollment plans align with most applicants, these played smaller roles compared to your Year 3 deciles. Overall, your profile stands out from typical admitted students due to that dip in later school rankings, despite strengths like your undergrad grades. This isn't a judgment on your potential as an Asian male pursuing law—many with similar backgrounds thrive—but the model weighs these patterns heavily from past data. To move forward, consider retaking courses or programs to boost your decile rankings, aiming for consistent top-half performance, and perhaps supplementing with bar prep experience. You might also explore schools with more holistic reviews or appeal with evidence of improvement since Year 3. With targeted steps, you can reshape how models like this see your bar exam readiness next time. Keep pushing; your above-average undergrad record shows you have the foundation to build on.
